enrichment.short_description: Captivate is a podcast hosting platform offering automatic distribution, IAB-certified analytics, monetization and AI show notes.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Captivate podcast hosting: distribution, IAB-certified analytics, monetization tools and a 30-day free trial. Compare plans and pricing.

features.title: Automatic Distribution
features.description: Distributes episodes to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Amazon Music, YouTube and other podcast apps.

features.title: IAB-Certified Analytics
features.description: Included on every plan, with performance comparison and listener retention metrics.

features.title: Monetization Tools
features.description: Membership subscriptions, tips, exclusive content and early access features for supporters.

features.title: Captivate Assistant
features.description: An AI tool that generates transcripts, titles, show notes and chapters.

features.title: Website Builder
features.description: A built-in podcast website is included free on every plan.

features.title: Private Podcasting
features.description: Dynamic ad insertion and private podcast feeds are included free on all plans.

features.title: Analytics Migration
features.description: Imports a show's complete download history from a previous host.

pros: IAB-certified analytics included on every plan, not gated to higher tiers
Built-in AI assistant handles transcripts, titles, show notes and chapters
30-day free trial gives full platform access with no feature restrictions
Dynamic ad insertion and private podcasting included free on all plans
25% discount for students, educational institutions, charities and nonprofits
cons: No permanent free plan; the site states it does not offer one
Pricing is shown in euros, which may complicate budgeting for non-EU customers
Download limits are tiered by plan, starting at 30,000/month
Usage beyond 300,000 downloads/month requires contacting sales for custom pricing

faqs.question: Does Captivate offer a free plan?
faqs.answer: No. Captivate states it does not have a permanently free tier, but every plan includes a 30-day free trial with full access.

faqs.question: What is included in every Captivate plan?
faqs.answer: All plans include dynamic ad insertion, distribution to major platforms, IAB-certified analytics, private podcasting, a website builder and promotional tools.

faqs.question: Does Captivate have AI tools?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Captivate Assistant generates transcripts, titles, show notes and chapters.

faqs.question: Is there a discount for nonprofits or students?
faqs.answer: Yes, students, educational institutions, registered charities and nonprofits receive 25% off all plans.

faqs.question: What happens if I exceed my plan's download limit?
faqs.answer: Customers exceeding 300,000 downloads/month on the Business plan need to contact Captivate's sales team for custom pricing.

use_cases.title: Independent podcasters
use_cases.description: Get analytics and monetization tools from day one.

use_cases.title: Podcast networks and agencies
use_cases.description: Manage multiple shows with team access.

use_cases.title: Podcasters switching hosts
use_cases.description: Migrate complete download history from a previous host.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Captivate?
paragraphs: Captivate is a podcast hosting and management platform that lets creators publish, distribute, grow and monetize podcasts. It automatically distributes episodes to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Amazon Music, YouTube and other podcast apps.

Every plan includes IAB-certified analytics, a built-in website, dynamic ad insertion, private podcasting and the Captivate Assistant, an AI tool that generates transcripts, titles, show notes and chapters.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Captivate is for
paragraphs: Captivate is built for entrepreneurs, content creators, YouTubers, podcast networks and agencies who want growth and monetization tools alongside hosting, rather than a bare-bones free host.

enrichment.short_description: Buzzsprout, founded in 2009, is podcast hosting with a free tier, AI-generated show notes and distribution to major podcast apps.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Buzzsprout podcast hosting: free 2-hour plan, AI-powered CoHost show notes, Magic Mastering audio, and distribution to Apple Podcasts and Spotify.

features.title: Audio + Video Hosting
features.description: Publishes audio and video episodes to major podcast directories.

features.title: CoHost AI
features.description: Generates show notes, transcripts, titles and chapters from an episode.

features.title: Magic Mastering
features.description: An AI-powered add-on that enhances episode audio quality.

features.title: IAB-Certified Analytics
features.description: Podcast statistics dashboard with filtering options.

features.title: Website Builder
features.description: SEO-optimized podcast websites with custom domain support.

features.title: Mobile App
features.description: iOS and Android apps for managing a show on the go.

features.title: Fan Mail
features.description: A tool for collecting listener feedback directly from episodes.

pros: Free plan available with 2 hours of uploads per month
Founded in 2009, with over 115,000 active podcasters on the platform
CoHost AI and Magic Mastering add-ons assist with content creation and audio quality
iOS and Android apps for on-the-go show management
3,600+ 5-star reviews cited by the company
cons: Free plan only retains episodes for 90 days and excludes video distribution
Video publishing requires the higher-priced Audio + Video plan
CoHost AI and Magic Mastering are paid add-ons, not included by default
The Archive plan still costs $5/month just to keep a podcast available without publishing

faqs.question: Does Buzzsprout have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers 2 hours of free uploads per month with episodes hosted for 90 days, though it excludes paid add-ons and video distribution.

faqs.question: When was Buzzsprout founded?
Does Buzzsprout support video podcasts?
faqs.answer: Yes, through the Audio + Video plan, which supports 1080p video distribution to Apple Podcasts.

faqs.question: What is CoHost AI?
faqs.answer: An AI feature that generates show notes, transcripts, titles and chapters for episodes.

faqs.question: Is there a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Buzzsprout offers iOS and Android apps for managing podcasts.

use_cases.title: New podcasters
use_cases.description: Test podcasting on the free plan before committing to a paid tier.

use_cases.title: Video podcasters
use_cases.description: Publish both audio and video episodes to directories like Apple Podcasts.

use_cases.title: Multi-show creators
use_cases.description: Run several podcasts under one account.

features: Keeps a podcast available without publishing new content

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Buzzsprout?
paragraphs: Buzzsprout, founded in 2009, is a podcast hosting and distribution platform that helps creators publish audio and video episodes to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, YouTube and other directories.
The platform offers a free plan with 2 hours of monthly uploads, plus paid tiers with AI tools like CoHost for show notes and transcripts, and Magic Mastering for audio enhancement.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Buzzsprout is for
paragraphs: Buzzsprout suits podcasters of all experience levels, from beginners starting on the free plan to established shows publishing audio and video across multiple podcasts.

enrichment.short_description: Podbean is a podcast hosting and monetization platform with a free tier, AI audio tools and IAB-certified analytics.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Podbean podcast hosting: free plan, AI-powered audio optimization and content generation, Ads Marketplace and custom podcast websites.

features.title: Podcast Hosting & Distribution
features.description: Publishes to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Amazon Music and other directories.

features.title: AI Audio Optimization
features.description: Noise reduction and filler-word removal powered by AI.

features.title: AI Content Generation
features.description: Generates titles, show notes, transcripts and chapter markers.

features.title: Monetization
features.description: Ads Marketplace and dynamic ad insertion for revenue.

features.title: Custom Website Templates
features.description: Customizable podcast website with templates.

features.title: IAB-Certified Analytics
features.description: Analytics dashboard for tracking audience growth.

features.title: Apple Podcasts Subscriptions
features.description: Native integration for paid subscriber content.

pros: Free account available to start podcasting
AI credits included on every paid plan for content generation and audio cleanup
Over 600,000 active podcasters cited, indicating a large, established user base
Network and Business plans support team collaboration and SSO for larger organizations
iOS and Android apps for recording and management
cons: AI features are credit-limited per plan (e.g. 600 credits on the entry paid tier)
Network-level stats and SSO require the most expensive plans
Monthly pricing is notably higher than annual billing across all paid tiers
Founding year and headquarters are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Podbean have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Podbean offers a free account with basic hosting features.

faqs.question: What are Podbean's AI credits used for?
faqs.answer: They power AI-based audio optimization and AI content generation like titles, show notes and transcripts.

faqs.question: Can I run a private podcast on Podbean?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Business plan supports private podcasts with up to 100 private members and SSO login.

faqs.question: Does Podbean support video podcasts?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Unlimited Plus plan and above support audio and video uploads.

faqs.question: Is there a mobile app?
faqs.answer: Yes, Podbean is available on web, iOS and Android.

use_cases.title: Hobbyist podcasters
use_cases.description: Start podcasting for free with basic hosting.

use_cases.title: Private business podcasts
use_cases.description: Run internal or member-only shows with SSO and team controls.

use_cases.title: Podcast networks
use_cases.description: Manage multiple public shows with team members.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Podbean?
paragraphs: Podbean is a podcast hosting platform that lets creators publish, promote and monetize podcasts, with distribution to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Amazon Music and other directories.
It combines a free tier with paid plans that add AI-powered audio optimization, AI content generation, an Ads Marketplace, and business features like SSO and private podcasting.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Podbean is for
paragraphs: Podbean serves podcasters of all experience levels and genres, as well as businesses seeking private podcasting with SSO and enterprise-grade security controls.

enrichment.short_description: Libsyn is a long-running podcast hosting platform with audio and video distribution, Pro Analytics and a 30-day free trial.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Libsyn podcast hosting: launch, host, distribute and monetize your show with Pro Analytics, multi-user access and a 30-day free trial.

features.title: Audio and Video Hosting
features.description: Distribution to Spotify, YouTube and Apple Podcasts.

features.title: Pro Analytics
features.description: IAB-certified download data with geo-trend information.

features.title: Multi-User Management
features.description: Supports up to five team members per account.

features.title: Monetization
features.description: Automatic ads, Podroll cross-promotion, and Apple Podcasts subscriptions.

features.title: Custom Podcast Player
features.description: Branded player and website for each show.

features.title: Bulk Episode Editing
features.description: Edit multiple episodes at once.

features.title: Video Uploads
features.description: Up to 100GB/month of video uploads included across all plans.

pros: 30-day free trial on every plan before billing starts
Video uploads (up to 100GB/month) included even on lower-tier plans
Multi-user management supports team collaboration for up to five users
Pro Analytics provides IAB-certified download data with geographic trends
Custom Professional and Enterprise plans available for higher-volume creators
cons: No permanent free plan, only a 30-day trial before paid billing
Audio upload hours are capped per plan (e.g. 3 hours/month on Basic)
Dynamic ad insertion and API access require custom Professional/Enterprise pricing
Founding year, headquarters and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Libsyn offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, every plan includes a 30-day free trial before billing begins, with no promo code required.

faqs.question: What audio upload limits does Libsyn have?
faqs.answer: The Basic plan allows 3 hours/month, Advanced allows 10 hours/month, and Max allows 55 hours/month.

faqs.question: Can I collaborate with a team on Libsyn?
faqs.answer: Yes, plans support multi-user access for up to five team members.

faqs.question: Does Libsyn support video podcasts?
faqs.answer: Yes, all plans include up to 100GB/month of video uploads with distribution to YouTube and Spotify.

faqs.question: Can I cancel a Libsyn trial anytime?
faqs.answer: Yes, the site states cancellation is available anytime.

use_cases.title: Teams with multiple hosts
use_cases.description: Collaborate with up to five team members on one account.

use_cases.title: Audio and video podcasters
use_cases.description: Distribute both audio and video episodes from one platform.

use_cases.title: High-volume creators
use_cases.description: Access dynamic ad insertion and API access on custom plans.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Libsyn?
paragraphs: Libsyn is a podcast hosting platform for launching, hosting, distributing and monetizing podcasts, with audio and video distribution to Spotify, YouTube and Apple Podcasts.
Every plan includes a 30-day free trial, Pro Analytics with IAB-certified downloads and geo-trend data, and multi-user management for teams of up to five.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Libsyn is for
paragraphs: Libsyn is built for ambitious, growth-focused creators who want detailed analytics, team collaboration, and monetization tools alongside standard podcast hosting.

enrichment.short_description: Simplecast is an end-to-end podcast hosting platform, now operating under AdsWizz, with a 14-day free trial.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Simplecast podcast hosting: one-click publishing, Audience by Simplecast analytics, AdsWizz ad monetization, and a 14-day free trial.

features.title: One-Click Publishing
features.description: Distributes episodes to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Pandora and more.

features.title: Audience by Simplecast
features.description: An IAB-certified analytics platform.

features.title: Ad Monetization
features.description: Ad inventory controls and access to the AdsWizz Marketplace.

features.title: Show Migration Tool
features.description: Imports an existing show from another host.

features.title: Multi-Show Management
features.description: Manage multiple shows from a single account.

features.title: API Integration
features.description: Available on the Enterprise plan for custom workflows.

features.title: Dynamic Ad Insertion
features.description: Available on the Professional and Enterprise plans.

pros: 14-day free trial with no credit card required
Operating for over 10 years, now part of AdsWizz
Basic and Essential plans include unlimited storage and uploads
Enterprise plan offers white-labeled RSS feeds and a dedicated success manager
Access to the AdsWizz ad marketplace for monetization
cons: No permanent free plan
Professional and Enterprise pricing is custom/quote-only, not published
Advanced analytics like location and unique-listener reports require the Essential plan or higher
Company headquarters and founding year are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Simplecast offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day commitment-free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Who operates Simplecast?
faqs.answer: The site states Simplecast now operates "By AdsWizz."

faqs.question: What's included in the Basic plan?
faqs.answer: 2 team seats, unlimited storage and uploads, distribution to all platforms, basic analytics, and 20,000 downloads included per month.

faqs.question: Does Simplecast support dynamic ad insertion?
faqs.answer: Yes, on the Professional and Enterprise plans.

faqs.question: Is API access available?
faqs.answer: Yes, on the Enterprise plan.

use_cases.title: Indie podcasters
use_cases.description: Get straightforward hosting and basic analytics.

use_cases.title: Growing networks
use_cases.description: Access advanced ad monetization through the AdsWizz Marketplace.

use_cases.title: Enterprises
use_cases.description: Use API access and white-labeled RSS feeds.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Simplecast?
paragraphs: Simplecast is a podcast hosting platform for publishing, distribution, analytics and monetization, offering one-click publishing to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, Pandora and other platforms.
The platform, which now operates under AdsWizz, provides IAB-certified analytics through Audience by Simplecast and ad monetization via the AdsWizz Marketplace.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Simplecast is for
paragraphs: Simplecast serves both growing podcast networks and indie creators, with entry-level plans for smaller shows and custom Professional/Enterprise plans for larger publishers.

enrichment.short_description: RedCircle is a podcast hosting and monetization platform with a permanently free Core plan and a cross-promotion marketplace.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: RedCircle podcast hosting: free unlimited Core plan, cross-promotions marketplace, dynamic ad insertion, and one-click video and transcripts.

features.title: Free Core Hosting
features.description: One podcast with unlimited storage, bandwidth, episodes and downloads.

features.title: Distribution
features.description: Publishes to Spotify, Apple Podcasts, Amazon Music, YouTube and more.

features.title: Cross-Promotions Marketplace
features.description: Pairs podcasts for mutual promotion, available on Growth plan and above.

features.title: RedCircle Ad Platform (RAP)
features.description: Programmatic and host-read ads for shows over 500 weekly downloads.

features.title: Video & Transcript Generation
features.description: One-click video and transcript creation from episodes.

features.title: Dynamic Insertion
features.description: Unlimited on the Growth plan, customizable on Pro.

features.title: Donations & Exclusive Content
features.description: Accepts listener donations and offers subscriber-only content.

pros: Core plan is permanently free with unlimited storage, bandwidth and downloads
Cross-promotions marketplace helps shows grow through partner promotion
Every paid plan includes a 7-day free trial
Ad monetization available on the free tier once a show hits 500 weekly downloads
Team seats available on Pro and Enterprise plans for collaboration
cons: Free Core plan is limited to a single podcast
Advanced analytics, video generation and higher transcript limits require paid plans
Enterprise-level features like a dedicated account manager require the $119.99/month plan
Founding year, headquarters and company size are not disclosed publicly

faqs.question: Is RedCircle free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Core plan is free and includes one podcast with unlimited storage, bandwidth, episodes and downloads.

faqs.question: How does RedCircle's ad program work?
faqs.answer: Shows with at least 500 weekly downloads can join the RedCircle Ad Platform for programmatic and host-read ads.

faqs.question: Does RedCircle offer a free trial on paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, each paid plan includes a 7-day free trial.

faqs.question: What is the cross-promotions marketplace?
faqs.answer: A feature on the Growth plan and above that pairs podcasts together to promote each other's shows.

faqs.question: How many episode transcripts are included?
faqs.answer: Growth includes 5/month, Pro includes 50/month, and Enterprise includes 500/month.

use_cases.title: Free unlimited hosting
use_cases.description: Start a podcast without any cost.

use_cases.title: Growth through cross-promotion
use_cases.description: Pair with other shows in the cross-promotions marketplace.
use_cases.audience: Growth-focused creators on the Growth plan

use_cases.title: Podcast teams
use_cases.description: Manage multiple podcasts with collaborator seats.
use_cases.audience: Podcast teams and agencies on Pro/Enterprise

long_form_blocks.heading: What is RedCircle?
paragraphs: RedCircle is a podcast hosting and monetization platform offering hosting, distribution, cross-promotions and host-read ad campaigns in one place.

Its Core plan is permanently free with unlimited storage, bandwidth and downloads for one podcast, while paid Growth, Pro and Enterprise plans add multi-podcast support, video generation and more transcripts.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who RedCircle is for
paragraphs: RedCircle suits new podcasters who want free, unlimited hosting, as well as growing shows that want to expand their audience through cross-promotion and ad monetization.

enrichment.short_description: RSS.com offers free, unlimited podcast hosting plus paid tiers with dynamic ad insertion, AI transcripts and audio-to-video conversion.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: RSS.com podcast hosting: free unlimited episodes and storage, automatic distribution, dynamic ad insertion, and AI-generated transcripts.

features.title: Unlimited Free Hosting
features.description: Unlimited episodes and audio storage on the free plan.

features.title: Automatic Distribution
features.description: Publishes to Spotify, Apple Podcasts, YouTube and Amazon Music.

features.title: Real-Time Analytics
features.description: Analytics dashboard included on all plans.

features.title: Monetization
features.description: Programmatic ads, subscriptions, donations, an affiliate program and crypto payments.

features.title: Dynamic Ad Insertion
features.description: Available from 10 downloads on paid plans.

features.title: AI-Generated Transcripts
features.description: Available on paid plans.

features.title: Audio-to-Video Conversion
features.description: Turns audio episodes into video for platforms like YouTube.

pros: Free plan includes unlimited episodes and unlimited audio storage
Dynamic ad insertion, subscriptions and donations available for monetization
Multiple payment options for monetization, including crypto payments
Site states certification by Podcast Standards and IAB Tech Lab
Site states existing podcasters switching hosts can get 6 months free
cons: AI transcripts, unbranded player and Apple Podcasts Subscriptions require a paid plan
Team member invitations are not available on the free plan
API access is limited to the top Podcast Networks plan
Founding year, headquarters and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Is RSS.com really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Local and Niche plan is free with unlimited episodes, unlimited audio, and basic analytics.

faqs.question: Does RSS.com support monetization?
faqs.answer: Yes, including dynamic ad insertion, Apple Podcasts Subscriptions, donations, and an affiliate program on paid plans.

faqs.question: Does RSS.com offer AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, AI-generated transcripts are included on paid plans.

faqs.question: Can I run multiple podcasts on RSS.com?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Podcast Networks plan supports unlimited podcasts and unlimited team seats.

faqs.question: Is there an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, API access is included on the Podcast Networks plan.

use_cases.title: Free niche hosting
use_cases.description: Launch a podcast with unlimited free hosting.

use_cases.title: Monetization-focused shows
use_cases.description: Add ads, subscriptions and AI transcripts.
use_cases.audience: Creators on the All in One Podcasting plan

use_cases.title: Podcast networks
use_cases.description: Manage multiple shows and team members with API access.
use_cases.audience: Networks on the Podcast Networks plan

long_form_blocks.heading: What is RSS.com?
paragraphs: RSS.com is a podcast hosting platform offering free, unlimited episode and storage hosting with automatic distribution to Spotify, Apple Podcasts, YouTube and Amazon Music.
Paid plans add dynamic ad insertion, Apple Podcasts Subscriptions, AI-generated transcripts and audio-to-video conversion, along with monetization options like donations and an affiliate program.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who RSS.com is for
paragraphs: RSS.com targets podcasters of all experience levels, from hobbyists wanting free unlimited hosting to networks needing multiple podcasts, team seats and API access.

enrichment.short_description: Castos is a podcast hosting platform with unlimited episodes, an AI show-notes assistant, WordPress integration and a 14-day trial.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Castos podcast hosting: unlimited episodes and shows, AI Assistant for show notes, WordPress integration, and a 14-day free trial.

features.title: Unlimited Hosting
features.description: Unlimited episodes, shows and downloads with no overage fees.

features.title: 1-Click Distribution
features.description: Publishes to Apple Podcasts, Spotify, YouTube and more.

features.title: Castos AI Assistant
features.description: Generates show notes, titles, social posts and chapter markers.

features.title: WordPress Integration
features.description: Connects via the Seriously Simple Podcasting plugin.

features.title: Hybrid Podcasting
features.description: Combines public and private content in one show.

features.title: Automated YouTube Republishing
features.description: Republishes episodes to YouTube automatically.

features.title: Private Podcasting App
features.description: iOS and Android app for private-podcast subscribers.

pros: Unlimited episodes, shows and downloads with no overage fees on every paid plan
14-day free trial with no credit card required
Native WordPress plugin integration for podcast websites
Transcripts available in 19 languages
Growth plan and above include a 1:1 onboarding call
cons: No permanent free plan; access requires starting a 14-day trial on a paid tier
Transcript volume is capped per plan, from 10/month on Essentials up to 100/month on Pro
Extra private subscribers cost $50/month per 500 beyond plan limits
Founding year, headquarters and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Does Castos have a free plan?
faqs.answer: No, but it offers a 14-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does Castos integrate with WordPress?
faqs.answer: Yes, via the Seriously Simple Podcasting plugin.

faqs.question: What does the Castos AI Assistant do?
faqs.answer: It generates show notes, titles, social posts and chapter markers.

faqs.question: Can I run a private podcast?
faqs.answer: Yes, Castos supports hybrid public and private podcasting with an iOS/Android app for subscribers.

faqs.question: How many languages are supported for transcripts?
faqs.answer: 19 languages.

use_cases.title: WordPress-based creators
use_cases.description: Publish podcasts through an existing WordPress site.

use_cases.title: Private/premium podcasts
use_cases.description: Offer subscriber-only content alongside a public feed.

use_cases.title: Growing shows
use_cases.description: Get onboarding support and automated video republishing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Castos?
paragraphs: Castos is a podcast hosting platform offering unlimited episodes, shows and downloads with 1-click distribution to Apple Podcasts, Spotify and YouTube.
The platform includes a Castos AI Assistant for generating show notes, titles, social posts and chapter markers, plus native WordPress integration via the Seriously Simple Podcasting plugin.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Castos is for
paragraphs: Castos suits podcasters who already publish through WordPress, shows that want a hybrid mix of public and private content, and growing creators who want onboarding support.

enrichment.short_description: Spreaker, an iHeartMedia company, is a podcast hosting platform with a permanently free plan and ad/subscription monetization.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Spreaker podcast hosting: free Free Speech plan, ad and subscription monetization, and Spreaker Create recording tools, owned by iHeartMedia.

features.title: Podcast Hosting & Publishing
features.description: Unlimited episodes with automatic distribution to major platforms.

features.title: Spreaker Create
features.description: A web-based recording and editing tool.

features.title: Prime Network
features.description: Featured placement for content within Spreaker's network.

features.title: Supporters Club
features.description: Listener subscription and support monetization.

features.title: Customizable RSS Feeds
features.description: Available from the free plan up.

features.title: Ad Monetization
features.description: Available on all plans, with subscription monetization on paid tiers.

features.title: Advanced Statistics
features.description: Extended stats history on paid plans, up to 24 months.

pros: Free Free Speech plan requires no credit card and includes ad monetization
Backed by iHeartMedia, a major media company
Supporters Club fee drops from 20% on the free plan to 0% on Broadcaster and above
Unlimited episodes included on every plan, including free
Podcasts app provides an additional discovery channel for listeners
cons: Free plan is limited to 1 podcast and 6 months of statistics history
Supporters Club takes a 20% fee on the free plan
Ad campaign manager for direct ad sales requires the top Publisher plan at €250/month
Founding year, headquarters and company size are not disclosed on the site

faqs.question: Is Spreaker free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free Speech plan is always free and requires no credit card.

faqs.question: Who owns Spreaker?
faqs.answer: Spreaker is "an iHeartMedia Company" per its site footer.

faqs.question: Does Spreaker charge fees on listener support?
faqs.answer: Yes, Supporters Club takes a 20% fee on the free plan, dropping to 0% on Broadcaster and above.

faqs.question: Can I sell ads directly through Spreaker?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Publisher plan includes an ad campaign manager for direct ad sales.

faqs.question: How many podcasts can I host?
faqs.answer: The free plan allows 1 podcast; Broadcaster and above allow unlimited podcasts.

use_cases.title: Free, ad-monetized shows
use_cases.description: Start a podcast at no cost with built-in ad monetization.

use_cases.title: 0%-fee listener support
use_cases.description: Keep 100% of Supporters Club revenue.

use_cases.title: Direct ad sales
use_cases.description: Sell ads directly to advertisers via a campaign manager.
use_cases.audience: Publishers and agencies on the Publisher plan

features: Ad campaign manager for direct ad sales

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Spreaker?
paragraphs: Spreaker is a podcast hosting and distribution platform, operated as "an iHeartMedia Company," that lets creators record, edit, publish and monetize podcasts.
It includes a permanently free Free Speech plan with ad monetization, plus paid tiers that add subscription monetization, unlimited podcasts, and a direct ad-sales campaign manager.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Spreaker is for
paragraphs: Spreaker suits podcasters of all levels, from hobbyists using the free plan to publishers running ad campaigns and selling ads directly through the Publisher plan.

enrichment.short_description: Megaphone is Spotify's enterprise podcast platform for publishing, dynamic ad insertion, and access to the Spotify Audience Network.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Megaphone by Spotify: enterprise podcast publishing, dynamic ad insertion, and access to the Spotify Audience Network ad marketplace.

features.title: Publishing
features.description: Distribution to virtually any podcast app.

features.title: Dynamic Ad Insertion

features.title: Spotify Audience Network
features.description: Access to Spotify's advertising marketplace.

features.title: Growth Analytics
features.description: Analytics and promotional tools for audience growth.

features.title: Enterprise Support
features.description: 24/7 support available for priority issues.

features.title: Standard Support
features.description: Live in-app and email support Monday-Friday, 4am-9pm ET.

pros: Backed by Spotify, giving priority access to Spotify's ad tools and roadmap
Access to the Spotify Audience Network advertising marketplace
Used by major publishers including Vox Media, ESPN, Sony Music, The Wall Street Journal and Paramount

Real-time dynamic ad insertion for monetization

faqs.question: Who owns Megaphone?
faqs.answer: Megaphone operates as a Spotify subsidiary/partner platform.

faqs.question: Is Megaphone pricing public?
faqs.answer: No, pricing is not disclosed on the site; prospective customers are directed to a pricing section to get in touch.

faqs.question: What is the Spotify Audience Network?
faqs.answer: An advertising marketplace that Megaphone customers can access for monetization.

faqs.question: Who uses Megaphone?
faqs.answer: The site lists examples including Vox Media, ESPN, Sony Music, The Wall Street Journal, Paramount and LinkedIn's Podcast Network.

faqs.question: What support does Megaphone offer?
faqs.answer: Live in-app and email support Monday-Friday 4am-9pm ET, plus 24/7 support for enterprise priority issues.

use_cases.title: Large media publishers
use_cases.description: Run enterprise-grade ad monetization across shows.

use_cases.title: Podcast networks
use_cases.description: Access the Spotify Audience Network for ad revenue.

use_cases.title: Ad-supported podcast businesses
use_cases.description: Use real-time dynamic ad insertion at scale.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Megaphone?
paragraphs: Megaphone is an enterprise podcast platform, operated by Spotify, that enables podcasters to publish, monetize and grow their audience across virtually any podcast app.
Its monetization tools include real-time dynamic ad insertion and access to the Spotify Audience Network, an advertising marketplace. Pricing is not published and requires contacting sales.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Megaphone is for
paragraphs: Megaphone is built for enterprise podcasters and networks; the site cites publishers such as Vox Media, ESPN, Sony Music, The Wall Street Journal and Paramount as examples.
